id: 2026-07-01-制作台按钮与顾客节奏优化
title: 制作台按钮与顾客节奏优化
goal: 优化制作台按钮交互，降低高峰期顾客排队压力，并补齐 Creator 预览验证

in: 优化制作台按钮交互，降低高峰期顾客排队压力，并补齐 Creator 预览验证
Clarify impacted gameplay, UI, data, and validation scope before coding.
Review gameplay flow, state transitions, and player-facing pacing around the requested behavior.
Clarify affected UI states, empty states, button affordances, and feedback timing.
Identify whether the request needs scene-authored nodes, script bindings, or preview-only adjustments.
Define concrete repo checks and any manual preview steps needed for acceptance.
out: Unrelated gameplay refactors outside the current request.
Large architecture rewrites unless explicitly required by the task.
Do not rewrite unrelated systems if a local behavior fix can solve the issue.
Do not restyle unrelated screens outside the requested flow.
Avoid broad scene cleanup unless it is necessary for the requested behavior.
Do not claim validation coverage for checks that were not actually run.

acceptance: Planner has written a concrete implementation direction and completion criteria.
Code, Review, QA, and Memory all have reserved sections in the same task card.
QA checklist includes repository-standard validation steps where applicable.
Gameplay behavior matches the intended pacing or rule change without breaking the existing loop.
UI state, visibility, and interactions remain consistent across the affected flow.
Scene references and runtime bindings stay valid in both script checks and Creator preview.
Validation steps and remaining gaps are explicitly recorded in the task card.
risks: Scene files and gameplay scripts can drift together and cause hidden regressions.
Manual Cocos preview may still be needed even when script-level validation passes.
Gameplay changes can silently affect income, queue pressure, energy, or recipe progression.
UI changes can drift from gameplay state and leave stale or misleading on-screen information.
Scene edits are easy to make visually but can introduce hidden binding regressions.
A change may look done while still lacking the checks needed to make it trustworthy.
qaChecks: Run validate:v1-loop when gameplay loop or UI flow changes.
Run typecheck:cocos when TypeScript scripts change.
Record any manual Creator preview findings in qa.checks.
implementationNotes: Prefer minimal, repo-aligned changes over introducing a parallel architecture.
Record deviations explicitly if implementation differs from the original plan.
Prefer adjusting the current gameplay flow and data hooks instead of introducing a parallel system.
Reuse existing UI builders and panel composition patterns before adding new structural layers.
Minimize scene churn and keep node-binding changes narrowly scoped.
Treat repository validation scripts and manual preview as first-class deliverables.

checks.name: Static review of changed files
checks.detail: Review changed scripts and scene/config impact before runtime validation.

checks.name: npm run validate:v1-loop
checks.detail: Run project loop validation after implementation if affected.

checks.name: npm run typecheck:cocos
checks.detail: Run Cocos TypeScript typecheck after implementation if scripts changed.

checks.name: Cocos preview smoke test
checks.detail: Verify core flow in Creator preview when UI, scene, or gameplay logic changes.

decisions: Planner draft auto-detected areas: gameplay, ui, scene, validation
nextDefaults: Use this task card as the single handoff source across Planner, Code, Review, QA, and Memory.

history.detail: Task created from request: 优化制作台按钮交互，降低高峰期顾客排队压力，并补齐 Creator 预览验证
